title: Alcohol intake documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports recording alcohol frequency, typical quantity, heavy-use episodes, dependence-related symptoms, harmful consequences, concern from others, and prior injury without automatically scoring or diagnosing.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact AUDIT domains support clinician-entered frequency, quantity, heavy-use episodes, dependence-related symptoms, harms, concern from others, and impact without automatic scoring or interpretation.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports recording alcohol frequency, typical quantity, heavy-use episodes, dependence-related symptoms, harmful consequences, concern from others, and prior injury without automatically scoring or diagnosing.

source.title: AUDIT: The Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test — Guidelines for Use in Primary Care, Second Edition

investigations.text: Supports documenting why alcohol use was reviewed and the primary-care screening context without asserting hazardous use.
investigations.rationale: The exact WHO manual supports documenting that alcohol use was reviewed in primary care without implying a positive screen or alcohol-related diagnosis.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting why alcohol use was reviewed and the primary-care screening context without asserting hazardous use.

source_limitations: Exact beverage, quantity, frequency, timing, standard-drink conversion, AUDIT responses, score, pregnancy, withdrawal, diagnosis, counselling, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
